summary: E2E testing complete for MULTIPLIKATOR Institut de Langues. Backend: 16/16 pytest tests passed (auth register/login/me/logout, contact CRUD with admin gating, catalog 44+ items with all required package_ids in XOF, Stripe checkout sessions + status + invalid-id 400, contact persistence verified via admin list). Frontend: All critical UI elements present and functional — hero CTAs, header (4 UPPERCASE tabs + S'inscrire), footer (MULTIPLIKATOR + INSTITUT DE LANGUAGES, WA CTA, Boutique M, campuses, privacy links), TrainingPlus grid (semi A1.1→B2.2 with course-buy data-testids + lang_c1_1→c2_2 cards 'sur devis' on row 3 + prep Goethe/ÖSD with -int/-ext buttons), Stripe checkout API called and returns 200 with valid url+session_id, /confidentialite + /traitement-donnees pages load, /team displays all 3 categories (Lambert SEHOUBO, Charles OBOSSOU, Administration/technique/Allemagne), /contact form POST /api/contact returns 200 + success toast displays, register (test+ui...@multiplikator.bj / Test1234!) redirects to /espace-apprenant, admin login redirects to /espace-apprenant. No bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend CORS allowed_origins is set to FRONTEND_URL only when CORS_ORIGINS='*' env var is used — current backend/.env has CORS_ORIGINS='*' which evaluates to single-element [FRONTEND_URL]. Works for the preview URL but is misleading naming; consider always parsing comma-separated list explicitly.
Backend /api/payments/checkout/session does NOT require authentication. Anyone can spam-create Stripe sessions. Consider rate-limiting or requiring an authenticated session for production.
/api/auth/google/session inserts a new user_sessions row on each call without de-duplicating by session_token (which has a unique index — could throw 500 on duplicate session). Consider upsert.
Contact endpoint stores PII (email, phone, message) without any rate-limit or CAPTCHA — production should add bot protection.
On register failure (duplicate email), backend returns 400 but frontend just shows alert — no inline error styling. Not blocking, just UX polish.

C1.1/C1.2/C2.1/C2.2 cards (lang_c1_*, lang_c2_*) on TrainingPlus correctly show 'sur devis' and have no buy button (intentional). Spec mentioned a buy button per card on row 3, but that conflicts with 'sur devis' pricing — main agent's implementation seems correct, this is just a clarification.
test_credentials: admin@multiplikator.bj / Multiplikator2026! (admin role). Test student dynamically created via /api/auth/register. RESEND_API_KEY is empty so email_sent:false on contact (expected per spec).
seed_data_creation: Several TEST_ prefixed users + contacts created via /api/auth/register and /api/contact during testing. They live in test_database collections (users, contacts, payment_transactions). Stripe sessions created are in test mode (cs_test_*).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All flows pass green. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable as regression. Use admin@multiplikator.bj / Multiplikator2026! for admin. For Stripe, the redirect intercept in headless Chrome via window.location.href setter override is tricky — instead listen to network response on POST /api/payments/checkout/session and verify response body contains url starting with https://checkout.stripe.com/ and session_id starting with cs_test_. Important testids on Contact page are contact-firstname / contact-lastname (NOT first-name with dash). TrainingPlus C1/C2 cards use lang_c1_1..lang_c2_2 ids (NOT semi_c*) and are price='sur devis' so have no buy button — by design.
